text: And when we talk about lobectomy, it may seem obvious, but we're talking about removing an anatomical lobe of the lung.
And the other thing we need to think about with this is if we're doing a lobectomy, it will be for cancer surgery most often.
And so we'll be doing some lymph node sampling.
But we can also use sharp dissection, quartery, things like harmonics are really, really useful in these situations for quick and easy dissection.
The next stage that is commonly done is dissection of the, identifying and dissecting the pulmonary vein, depending on what lobe you're going to.
So it's trying to prevent that spread of tumour through the port incision site.
Some people choose to put an extra site so that you get good closure of that uniportal port site.
